sponsors.name: Phantom
sponsors.content:
Phantom Connect is the recommended embedded wallet for all Frontier hackathon projects. Ship wallet functionality in minutes — with seamless email sign-in for web2 users and native support for existing Phantom users — at no cost.

- [Get Started](https://docs.phantom.com/phantom-connect)
- [Integration Guide](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_TSqD9wTO-VQMUoUqMQZ8TSoxVx5YuWz/view)
- [React Starter Template](https://github.com/Th3Ya0vi/phantom-embedded-react-starter)
- [JS Template](https://github.com/Th3Ya0vi/phantom-embedded-js)
- [React Native Template](https://github.com/Th3Ya0vi/phantom-embedded-react-native-starter)

Stop paying for auth and wallet infrastructure. Phantom Connect gives your project production-grade wallet UX, email onboarding for web2 users, and native support for existing crypto users — all with a few lines of code. Also ready for agentic use cases via the Phantom MCP Server.

In addition, CASH is the recommended stablecoin for Frontier hackathon transactions and prize payouts. Backed by Phantom, purpose-built for Solana. Integrate a stablecoin that your users already hold in Phantom and is accepted across Stripe's global merchant network. Reduce friction for in-app payments, tipping, and commerce flows. CASH also lets you keep 100% of the net revenue generated from the supply you originate.

- [Get Started with CASH](https://docs.phantom.com/cash)

sponsors.name: Altitude
sponsors.content:
Multisig secures what you build. Altitude runs the business.

Both products are built by Squads. Five years of financial infrastructure on Solana, securing over $15B in tokenized value.

Squads Multisig is Solana's premier multisig platform. Secure your program upgrade authorities, project tokens, and validator keys with multi-party approval. Over 500 of Solana's leading teams trust Multisig to secure their Solana assets, including Helius, Jito, Kamino, Jupiter, MetaDAO and more.

- [Get Started with Multisig](https://squads.xyz/multisig)

Altitude is the financial operating system for businesses on the frontier. Secure your stablecoin treasury, spend with cards, earn APY on balances, and move money globally with ACH, SEPA and SWIFT.

Altitude is how Solana's leading teams run their financial operations.

Learn more:

- [Altitude Website](https://altitude.xyz)
- [Altitude on X](https://x.com/altitude)

sponsors.name: World
sponsors.content:
IDKit is how you embed Proof of Human into your product. Proofs are zero-knowledge and unlinkable across apps, so your users can verify their World ID without revealing any information about themselves. To get started, you need to: install the SDK, register the app in the Developer Portal, generate a backend signature to prevent forgery, collect the proof from the user, and verify it server-side.

- [IDKit integration guide](https://docs.world.org/world-id/idkit/integrate)

MiniKit 2.0 enables you to deploy your app inside the World App and tap into our distribution. You can load docs.world.org/llms-full.txt into your AI coding tool for full MiniKit context.

- [MiniKit 2.0 quick start docs](https://docs.world.org/mini-apps/quick-start/installing)

AgentKit lets you register an agent wallet against a verified World ID, then use that signal on your side to grant preferred access or offer discounts to human-backed agents. It accepts payments on both World Chain and Base using x402.

- [AgentKit integration docs](https://docs.world.org/agents/agent-kit/integrate)

sponsors.name: Arcium
sponsors.content:
Arcium brings encrypted computation to blockchains, making it possible to build applications where data remains private by default. This opens up a new design space beyond transparent smart contracts, enabling private DeFi, encrypted AI, and entirely new market primitives.

Using Arcis, Arcium's Rust framework, developers can quickly build and deploy encrypted applications. In the hackathon, we encourage teams to explore this design space, whether that's dark pools, private perps and lending, blind auctions, encrypted voting, raffles and lotteries, or entirely new market primitives such as opportunity markets. Arcium also enables primitives like threshold signing and secure randomness, along with capabilities such as access control.

- [Documentation](https://docs.arcium.com/)
- [Developer Documentation](https://docs.arcium.com/developers)
- [Purple Paper](https://www.arcium.com/articles/arcium-purplepaper)
- [Request for Products](https://www.arcium.com/articles/request-for-products)

sponsors.name: Reflect
sponsors.content:
Reflect is credibly-neutral stablecoin software, where the software consists of decentralized financial strategies executed and managed permissionlessly by programs rather than humans. Reflect then tokenizes these strategies in the form of interest-bearing US Dollars (stablecoins).

- [Docs](https://docs.reflect.money/)
- [@reflectmoney/stable.ts](https://npmjs.com/package/@reflectmoney/stable.ts)

sponsors.name: Pentagon
sponsors.content:
Pentagon is how you build your Frontier hackathon team and move faster. Go from solo to running a fleet of agents in minutes - they work in parallel, talk to one another, and run on your existing Claude Code subscription. Task your agents with engineering, marketing, customer support, and everything in between.

- [Get Started](https://docs.pentagon.run/quickstart)
- [Documentation](https://docs.pentagon.run/introduction)
- [FAQ](https://docs.pentagon.run/reference/faq)

Pentagon is a native macOS workspace where your AI agents communicate, coordinate, and ship as a team. See your whole team on a visual canvas - who's active, who's waiting, what needs your attention. Define communication patterns and watch agents coordinate and ship autonomously. If you need inspiration, start with our gstack template - 34 agents across frontend, backend, infrastructure, QA, and docs powered by Garry Tan's GStack skills.

- [Download Pentagon for free today](https://pentagon.run/download)

sponsors.name: MoonPay
sponsors.content:
MoonPay Agents is infrastructure for AI agents to move money. Agents can create wallets, hold assets, and execute on-chain transactions. Agents have access to all MoonPay tools like On/Off-ramps, virtual accounts, commerce, cross-chain swaps, virtual cards, etc.

During Frontier, we'd like to see agents move money both in the real world and agentic economy. We want to see experiences that give agents bank accounts or virtual cards to participate in commerce, see new DeFi experiences using stablecoins, etc.

With one command: `npm install -g @moonpay/cli` and our skills repo, users can get 30+ skills to start building.

sponsors.name: Coinbase
sponsors.description: Coinbase Developer Platform resources for x402 pay-per-request APIs, agent wallets, onramps/offramps, and wallet infrastructure.
sponsors.content:
Coinbase Developer Platform gives Frontier teams a practical stack for agentic commerce: x402 for paid APIs, CDP facilitator infrastructure, Agentic Market service discovery, onramps/offramps, and wallet products for users, backends, and autonomous agents.

x402 lets builders charge for APIs, data, tools, and content over HTTP with instant stablecoin payments. The CDP facilitator handles payment verification and settlement, while the x402 MCP server shows how agents can call paid APIs without manual checkout.

- [x402 Docs](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/x402/welcome)
- [x402 MCP Server](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/x402/mcp-server)
- [Agentic Market](https://agentic.market/)

For product flows that need funding or custody, Coinbase Onramp & Offramp support fiat-to-crypto funding and crypto-to-fiat cash out. The wallet guide helps teams choose between embedded wallets, server wallets, and agentic wallets; the Agentic Wallet Skills repo gives agents prebuilt actions for funding, sending USDC, trading, finding x402 services, and paying for them.

- [Onramp & Offramp](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/onramp/introduction/welcome)
- [Wallet Product Guide](https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/server-wallets/comparing-our-wallets)
- [Agentic Wallet Skills](https://github.com/coinbase/agentic-wallet-skills)

sponsors.name: Metaplex
sponsors.content:
Metaplex is building the agent exchange for Solana. With the Metaplex Agent Kit, 014 registry, and agent token launchpad, Metaplex is providing the infrastructure for agents to be discoverable and economically viable onchain.

The 014 registry enables developers to register agents onchain as Core NFTs that come with built-in wallets. From there, agents can launch tokens, mint and own NFTs, and participate in agent-to-agent commerce. During Frontier, we encourage teams to build useful agents: production-ready agents with identity, ownership, and economic functionality built in from day one.

- [Register an Agent](https://developers.metaplex.com/agents/register-agent)
- [Run an Agent](https://developers.metaplex.com/agents/run-an-agent)

A simple starting point is to register your agent on Solana using the Metaplex Agent Kit, connect your runtime, and fund the agent wallet so it can begin transacting or deploying programs. From there, builders can launch tokens, coordinate with other agents, or create new agent-driven marketplaces and services.

- [Launch a Token](https://www.metaplex.com/docs/agents/create-agent-token)
- [Metaplex Skill](https://www.metaplex.com/docs/agents/skill)
- [Agent Registry](https://metaplex.com/agents)
- [Agent Kit Docs](https://metaplex.com/docs/agents)

Teams that incorporate identity, discoverability, and economic functionality will be best positioned to build durable agent applications. Metaplex DevRel support and office hours are available for all Frontier hackathon teams.

sponsors.name: Swig
sponsors.content:
Swig is building the future of smart wallets for agents, consumers, and enterprises. Our open-source SDKs and APIs enable deep programmability with a robust onchain policy engine, gasless payments, delegated execution, and zk identity. Swig was designed for developers with a low CU, trust-minimized architecture, and is used by leading agentic finance apps, crypto neobanks, consumer DeFi apps, and institutions.

What to build with Swig: Programmable smart wallets are well suited for several use cases: agentic payments, automated DeFi strategies, web2-style consumer apps, real-world payments, corporate treasury, or institutional finance.

- [Overview](https://build.onswig.com/)
- [TypeScript SDK Tutorial](https://build.onswig.com/tutorials/typescript)
- [TypeScript SDK](https://github.com/anagrambuild/swig-ts)
- [Rust SDK](https://github.com/anagrambuild/swig-wallet)
- [Developer Portal APIs](https://dashboard.onswig.com/)
- [Developer Portal Example](https://build.onswig.com/examples/dev-portal)

sponsors.name: LI.FI
sponsors.content:
LI.FI is universal liquidity infrastructure for Solana. Integrate once to power cross-chain functionality in your app — Solana swaps aggregating Jupiter, DFlow, Titan, OKX, and Fly for best execution, bridging to and from Solana across 20+ bridges and 60+ chains, and seamless cross-chain asset exchanges. A single API integration gives you access to all major DEXs, bridges, and solvers.

- [LI.FI Docs](https://docs.li.fi/)
- [Solana Overview](https://docs.li.fi/li.fi-api/solana)
- [REST API Reference](https://docs.li.fi/api-reference/introduction)

LI.FI also provides tooling for AI agent development — an MCP Server for cross-chain swap functionality, agent skills on GitHub, and an Openclaw skill for bridging and swapping across 35+ chains.

- [MCP Server](https://docs.li.fi/mcp-server/overview)
- [Agent Skills](https://github.com/lifinance/lifi-agent-skills)

sponsors.name: Vanish
sponsors.content:
Vanish is a privacy layer for Solana that lets traders execute without exposing their strategy on-chain and without compromising compliance. It breaks the link between a public wallet and on-chain activity while preserving full control over routing and execution, and integrates directly into existing swap flows with no impact on performance or UX.

- [Developer Docs](https://core.vanish.trade)
- [API Reference](https://core.vanish.trade/api-reference)

Vanish is offering $10,000 to the team that builds the best integration using the Vanish Core API. Build private swaps in places they haven't existed before — Telegram trading bots that swap without linking wallet history, portfolio rebalancing tools that rotate positions without revealing strategy, AI agents that execute trades without exposing the agent's wallet, or payment flows where merchants accept tokens privately. Private trading with Vanish adds ~200ms to trade finality, works with any DEX aggregator, and requires no changes to your existing RPC setup.

sponsors.name: Privy
sponsors.description: Embedded wallet infrastructure for onboarding users and building full onchain experiences.
sponsors.content:
Privy provides embedded wallet infrastructure that lets you onboard users and build full onchain experiences without managing wallets yourself. With a single integration, you can create self-custodial wallets for every user and enable seamless interaction with Solana apps. This goes from funding and swaps to DeFi participation, all within your product.

- [Docs](https://docs.privy.io)
- [Agent CLI + Sandbox](https://agents.privy.io)

For the Colosseum hackathon, we're especially excited to see teams go beyond basic wallet creation and showcase end-to-end onchain flows. Use Privy to power experiences like in-app swaps, earning on idle balances, or gasless transactions that abstract away friction for users. Whether you're building a consumer app, trading product, or fintech experience on Solana, Privy gives you the primitives to make it feel fast, seamless, and invisible.

sponsors.name: Condor
sponsors.description: Open-source agentic harness for performing trades and building trading agents.
sponsors.content:
Condor is a new agentic harness from Hummingbot Foundation, similar in design to OpenClaw. It connects to your LLM to help you perform trades and create trading agents that execute algorithmic trading strategies on Solana DEXs, centralized exchanges, and perp DEXs.

- [Intro Blog Post](https://hummingbot.org/blog/introducing-condor-the-open-source-harness-for-trading-agents/)
- [Documentation](https://condor.hummingbot.org/)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/hummingbot/condor)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/hummingbot)

Condor is open source and free to use, and is built and maintained by Hummingbot Foundation. Starting May 19, Hummingbot is hosting a one-month hackathon with $15K+ in prizes for the best trading agents built in Condor.

- [Condor Agents Hackathon](https://www.botcamp.xyz/hackathons/condor-agents-hackathon)

Stablecoin rails backed by Phantom for payouts, in-app payments, and commerce flows.
Programmable wallets with policy controls, gasless payments, and delegated execution.

Stablecoin treasury ops with cards, yield, and global money movement.
AI-powered vulnerability scanner for automated blackbox and whitebox web app penetration testing.

rpcProviders.name: Helius
rpcProviders.description: Helius is Solana's leading RPC and data infrastructure provider, offering globally distributed nodes with sub-millisecond response times, staked connections for faster transaction confirmations, and exclusive archival methods like getTransactionsForAddress. Helius also provides LaserStream — the fastest way to stream Solana blocks, transactions, and account data via gRPC — and enhanced WebSockets for real-time on-chain event streaming.
rpcProviders.offer: 50% off Developer plans for Frontier builders ($49 → $24.50/mo)

rpcProviders.name: Triton One
rpcProviders.description: Triton One provides high-performance bare-metal RPC servers for Solana applications. Triton specializes in dedicated servers and custom deployments. Triton also offers a global shared RPC pool perfect for web3 front ends. Call Triton when you're ready to scale up. Triton's private devnet and testnet are always free.

rpcProviders.name: FluxRPC
rpcProviders.description: FluxRPC is a high-performance Solana RPC provider with Yellowstone gRPC streaming support and Lantern — a local edge-caching layer for Solana account state that delivers sub-millisecond reads at 10,000+ requests per second. Pay only for bandwidth ($0.06/GB), making it one of the most cost-effective RPC options available for high-volume applications.
rpcProviders.offer: Developer plan at $15/month (limited-time) with access to Yellowstone streams, Lantern local RPC for faster data recall and performance, and low-latency RPC across high-volume calls.
